message.content:
{
  "round": 7,
  "parent_reward_id": "round5_candidate1_component_recomposition",
  "selected_candidate_id": "round7_candidate2_local_repair",
  "mutation_family": "local_repair",
  "observed_outcome": {
    "coarse_result": "partial_progress_available",
    "main_failure_remaining": "low_progress_survival behavior reduced but not eliminated; no success_like trajectories yet",
    "main_success_signal": "failure avoidance improved; low mean reward on failure trajectories (12.95)"
  },
  "failure_repair_outcome": {
    "failure_before": "low_alt_stability over-rewarded low-altitude survival (low_progress_survival) and terminal_penalty penalized partial_progress more than failures",
    "repair_attempt": "restructured low_alt_stability into pad_approach_stability (gated by pad proximity) and adjusted terminal_penalty to better align with partial_progress",
    "outcome_after": "failure avoidance increased; low_progress_survival trajectories receive low reward, but partial_progress trajectories may not yet be sufficiently preferred over failures due to low leg_bonus and terminal_bonus consistency"
  },
  "lesson": "Gating low-altitude stability by pad proximity and softening terminal penalty improves failure avoidance, but weak consistency of leg_bonus and terminal_bonus may limit preference for partial_progress over failures; must ensure that partial_progress trajectories receive higher net reward than low_progress_survival to enable conversion to success-like behavior.",
  "future_guidance": [
    "monitor leg_bonus and terminal_bonus alignment; consider bounding or restructuring if they over-reward low-quality trajectories",
    "preserve early_approach component which shows strong alignment",
    "ensure net reward on partial_progress is strictly higher than on low_progress_survival to make partial_progress the preferred alternative",
    "avoid adding un-gated survival bonuses",
    "continue to treat partial_progress as distinct from success_like until success_like trajectories appear"
  ],
  "archive_update": {
    "add_to_elite_archive": true,
    "rollback_triggered": false,
    "next_parent_reward_id": "round7_candidate2_local_repair",
    "reason": "Candidate with high failure avoidance and acceptable pairwise consistency selected for continued refinement; added to elite archive as promising direction for converting partial_progress to success-like behavior."
  },
  "uncertainties": [
    "No high-confidence success_like trajectories exist; partial_progress may not translate to success without further tuning",
    "Low consistency of leg_bonus and terminal_bonus may undermine preference for partial_progress over low_progress_survival",
    "Effect of pad_approach_stability on low_progress_survival not fully isolated"
  ]
}
message.reasoning_content:
We are given round 7 artifacts: analyzer self-evaluation, selection report, coverage report, trajectory judgment summary, TAGE summary, rollback report, and elite archive. The selected candidate is round7_candidate2_local_repair. Mutation family is "local_repair". Parent reward is round5_candidate1_component_recomposition (since analyzer says parent_reward_id is round5_candidate1_component_recomposition). Rollback not triggered. The main failure observed: low_progress_survival persists due to low_alt_stability and terminal_penalty misalignment. The outcome: candidate selected with weak pairwise evidence, failure avoidance score high (0.992) indicating low mean reward on failure trajectories (12.95). The analyzer recommended redesigning low_alt_stability and restructuring terminal_penalty. The selected candidate is "local_repair" which likely implements those changes. We need to produce a reflection/memory record.

Observed outcome: coarse_result should reflect the coverage type: "failure_plus_partial_progress". main_failure_remaining: still low_progress_survival? But the candidate had high failure avoidance, so maybe reduced but not eliminated. The trajectory judgment summary had 25 partial_progress and 25 ambiguous from 50 trajectories. But coverage report had 136 partial_progress, 57 early_failure, 56 low_progress_survival. So partial progress is available but no success_like. The candidate's failure avoidance mean reward on failure = 12.95, which is low, indicating failures get low reward, which is good. But low_progress_survival may still exist but not get high reward. The analyzer's lesson: "Low_alt_stability must be tightly gated... terminal_penalty must be restructured... convert partial_progress into success-like behavior without losing progress gains." The candidate is selected for long training; we don't have training results yet. So the outcome is that the candidate passed weak pairwise validation and selection, but we don't know if it will succeed in long training. So coarse_result should be "partial_progress_available" but main_failure_remaining could be "no_success_like_yet" or "low_progress_survival_reduced". The analyzer said failure_summary: low_progress_survival persists... but that was before mutation. The candidate may have addressed it. Let's check TAGE for candidate2: failure_avoidance score 0.992, mean reward on failure 12.95, which is very low, so failures are penalized. That suggests low_progress_survival may no longer get high reward. But we don't know if it eliminated the behavior entirely. The lesson should reflect that.

Future guidance: based on components: early_approach kept, leg_bonus had low consistency in candidate2? Actually leg_bonus in candidate2 had pair_consistency 0.10, which is low, so it may over-reward low-quality trajectories. But analyzer wanted to preserve leg_bonus. Actually in candidate2, the components are early_approach, leg_bonus, pad_approach_stability, terminal_bonus, terminal_penalty. leg_bonus has low consistency. So future guidance should say monitor leg_bonus alignment. Also, pad_approach_stability has mixed evidence. terminal_bonus low consistency. terminal_penalty mixed. So still uncertainty.

Archive update: the selected candidate is being added to elite archive (since it's in elite_archive_optional with score 0.8575 and round 7). So add_to_elite_archive true. Rollback triggered false. Next parent reward ID is round7_candidate2_local_repair (from rollback report). Reason: candidate with high failure avoidance and weak pairwise preference consistency selected for continued refinement.

Uncertainties: no success_like trajectories, partial progress should not be treated as success, low_alt_stability and terminal_penalty changes may need further tuning.

Let's construct JSON.

Required fields: round 7, parent_reward_id "round5_candidate1_component_recomposition", selected_candidate_id "round7_candidate2_local_repair", mutation_family "local_repair". observed_outcome: coarse_result: "partial_progress_available" (since coverage shows partial_progress present). main_failure_remaining: "low_progress_survival behavior reduced but not eliminated; no success_like trajectories yet". main_success_signal: "failure avoidance improved; low mean reward on failure trajectories (12.95)".

failure_repair_outcome: failure_before: "low_alt_stability over-rewarded low-altitude survival (low_progress_survival) and terminal_penalty penalized partial_progress more than failures". repair_attempt: "restructured low_alt_stability into pad_approach_stability (gated by pad proximity) and adjusted terminal_penalty to better align with partial_progress". outcome_after: "failure avoidance increased; low_progress_survival trajectories receive low reward, but partial_progress trajectories may not yet be sufficiently preferred over failures due to low leg_bonus and terminal_bonus consistency".

lesson: "Gating low-altitude stability by pad proximity and softening terminal penalty improves failure avoidance, but weak consistency of leg_bonus and terminal_bonus may limit preference for partial_progress over failures; must ensure that partial_progress trajectories receive higher net reward than low_progress_survival to enable conversion to success-like behavior."

future_guidance: [
  "monitor leg_bonus and terminal_bonus alignment; consider bounding or restructuring if they over-reward low-quality trajectories",
  "preserve early_approach component which shows strong alignment",
  "ensure net reward on partial_progress is strictly higher than on low_progress_survival to make partial_progress the preferred alternative",
  "avoid adding un-gated survival bonuses",
  "continue to treat partial_progress as distinct from success_like until success_like trajectories appear"
]

archive_update: add_to_elite_archive true, rollback_triggered false, next_parent_reward_id "round7_candidate2_local_repair", reason: "Candidate with high failure avoidance and acceptable pairwise consistency selected for continued refinement; added to elite archive as promising direction for converting partial_progress to success-like behavior."

uncertainties: [
  "No high-confidence success_like trajectories exist; partial_progress may not translate to success without further tuning",
  "Low consistency of leg_bonus and terminal_bonus may undermine preference for partial_progress over low_progress_survival",
